description: This set of transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images shows post-tensile deformed microstructures of the 28Mn0Ni Ni-free Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steel alloy. Subfigure (a) corresponds to the sample processed via hot rolling (HR), cold rolling (CR) and annealing at 973 K, deformed to a true strain ε~0.14, which exhibits dislocation multiplication and dislocation cell formation. Subfigures (b) to (e) correspond to samples processed via HR+CR and annealing at 1173 K, deformed to true strains of ~0.02 (b), ~0.08 (c) and ~0.30 (d, e) respectively. Subfigures (b) to (d) reveal progressive slip band refinement with increasing applied strain, while subfigure (e) shows interaction between dislocations and pre-existing annealing twins. All subfigures have a 200 nm scale bar. The figure supports the conclusion that the observed microstructural evolution processes act as the core strain hardening mechanisms for the alloy.
